title: chilocalyx maculatus
intro: Chilocalyx maculatus, also known as the spotted horned tree, is a species of plant in the family Santalaceae, commonly found in Western Australia. The leaves of the Chilocalyx maculatus plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and act as an antiseptic. Chilocalyx maculatus is often prepared as a tea or infused oil, and can also be used as a topical application to aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions.
